---
title: "About the Data"
subtitle: "Where these numbers come from, and what they mean"
toc: true
---

## The short version

Every figure on this site is fetched directly from the **New Jersey Department of
Education** using the open-source [`njschooldata`](https://github.com/almartin82/njschooldata)
R package. There is no hand-entered data, no estimation, and no fabrication. If a
number isn't published by the state, the section says so rather than guessing.

## Data sources

| Section | Source | Years shown |
|---|---|---|
| Enrollment & demographics | NJ DOE Fall Enrollment files | 2015–2026 |
| Spending | [Taxpayers' Guide to Educational Spending](https://www.nj.gov/education/guide/) (TGES) | 2014–2024 (actuals) |
| Graduation | NJ DOE Four-Year Adjusted Cohort Graduation Rate | 2013–2025 |
| High-school proficiency | NJGPA (NJ Graduation Proficiency Assessment) | 2022–2025 |
| Chronic absenteeism | NJ School Performance Reports | 2019–2024 |
| Staffing ratios | NJ School Performance Reports | latest year |

## How to read the comparisons

Most charts compare a district to its **peer group median** (the dashed line).
Peers are defined by **District Factor Group (DFG)** — the NJ DOE's
socioeconomic classification of districts, lettered A (highest need) through J
(most affluent). Comparing a district to demographically similar districts is far
more meaningful than comparing it to the statewide average. Charter and special
districts without a DFG are compared against all districts.

Percentiles ("72nd percentile among 103 DFG I peers") describe where a district
falls within its peer group on that metric, from the latest available year.

## Caveats and suppression

- **Small cells are suppressed at the source.** The NJ DOE withholds counts for
  very small groups to protect student privacy; these appear as missing, never as
  zero or an estimate.
- **Spending is district-level.** TGES reports traditional districts, not
  individual charters, so charter profiles omit the spending section.
- **Per-pupil spending uses "actuals."** Figures lag ~2 years behind the budget
  year, consistent with how TGES reports settled costs.
- **Some districts don't enroll every grade.** A K–8 district has no graduation
  rate; a profile marks such sections as not applicable rather than showing an
  empty chart.

## Reproduce any number

Everything here is one function call away:

```r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("almartin82/njschooldata")
library(njschooldata)

fetch_enr(2026, tidy = TRUE)        # enrollment, all districts
fetch_grad_rate(2025)               # graduation rates
fetch_tges(2024)                    # spending (Taxpayers' Guide)
fetch_absence(2024, level = "district")  # chronic absenteeism
```

The full data engine — 80+ fetchers covering assessments, finance, staffing,
discipline, and more — is documented at
[almartin82.github.io/njschooldata](https://almartin82.github.io/njschooldata/).

## Who made this

Built by [Andy Martin](https://github.com/almartin82) on top of `njschooldata`,
the open interface to New Jersey school data. Found an error? Open an issue on
[GitHub](https://github.com/almartin82/njschooldata/issues).
